reviews.review_msg: New to teaching. Not organized. Very young and quite in to her research. Changed the outline too much. Assignment expectations were not clear and led her to be an unfair marker. Not sure what she wanted from us. Course was okay.

She is disorganized and spends an EXCESSIVE amount of time discussing her own research. She  attempts to discuss material she is not prepared to explain. Assignment expectations were unclear. The student presentations were the only fun part. I would not recommend this instructor until she develops her teaching skills.

The most unorganized prof ever, however her assignments are easy and surprisingly enjoyable. Her lectures usually always comprise of her talking about her work in the down town east side. Barley go over readings and she rarley follows the syllabus she gives out. Great person and easy marker, but seems to busy with her discertation to teach properly

New to teaching. Not organized. Very young and quite in to her research. Changed the outline too much. Assignment expectations were not clear and led her to be an unfair marker. Not sure what she wanted from us. Course was okay.

The student presentations were the only fun part. I would not recommend this instructor until she develops her teaching skills.

Barley go over readings and she rarley follows the syllabus she gives out. Great person and easy marker, but seems to busy with her discertation to teach properly

Nice person, theory driven, did give a lot of examples. Funny. making the online course really a community of support. SHe treats students as peers, and give you a lot of space for personal voices. like her.
Passionate prof, flexible and easy going, she can sometimes funny. I like the marking scheme. she treats students almost as peers and allows you to talk and present a lot. Will take her again.

Jie is really smart and very informative however she is a terrible professor. In 201, we were required to facilitate a 2 HOUR presentation in pairs and the mark for this presentation was lumped into our participation grade worth a total of only 15%. Her tests are extremely obscure and very long. I would never willingly take another class with her
I really like her teaching styles, open and flexibile. Tests are all open book no need to memorize anything, but you have to come to class to really understand those concepts. fair marker

Jie treats you as an equal instead of as a student, as long as you pay attention. Assignments were easy and open-ended, however it's VERY important that you clarify what she's looking for. SA201 is all about discussions, so you'd better be familiar with the big topics in humanities (like Neoliberalism) because she didn't explain them in depth.

Jie is nice and is interested in what she teaches, but NEVER again will I take a class with her.  She makes you feel stupid when you ask her a question.  Not only does she give you a look(which makes me feel stupid), but she also doesn't know how to explain her answers.  She expects you to get her right away.  She rarely respond to your email
I really like Jie. She is such a pleasant, enthusiastic person. I like all her China examples and her fieldwork stories which make this theory course very interesting. she is a fair marker too.
Love her class. Theory driven but her teaching made me think.

Jie is great teacher. very enthusiastic about theories and good at teaching theories. And also she has a great personality, very pleasant and optimistic. She could easily come up with some moving anecdotes to cheer you up. But some of her theories are very updated and may take a while to understand, but she reviewed alot.

Would not recommend her as a professor. She knows her stuff but can't teach it. She took things personally, and was on a short fuse most of the time. I think she may become a better prof in the future but she hasn't been teaching long enough. Take her classes only if you are very patient and don't expect to learn much!

Nice person, theory driven, did give a lot of examples. Funny. making the online course really a community of support. SHe treats students as peers, and give you a lot of space for personal voices. like her.
Passionate prof, flexible and easy going, she can sometimes funny. I like the marking scheme. she treats students almost as peers and allows you to talk and present a lot. Will take her again.

I think she may become a better prof in the future but she hasn't been teaching long enough. Take her classes only if you are very patient and don't expect to learn much!

Pamela is a pretty brutal professor. Her assignment instructions are super vague, and those which seem like they should be worth more aren't and vice versa. She puts a 10% emphasis on participation, yet responds rudely ("oh.." "ok" or just simply "no that's wrong") when you participate. Very frustrating professor.

Pamela is a pretty brutal professor. Her assignment instructions are super vague, and those which seem like they should be worth more aren't and vice versa. She puts a 10% emphasis on participation, yet responds rudely ("oh.." "ok" or just simply "no that's wrong") when you participate. Very frustrating professor.
Very helpful and fair professor. Highly recommended.

She really likes her material, and is a nice person. But tbh her class is ridiculous. The TA was incredibly hard marker, you get no information on how to do anything, and nothing she seemed to talk about actually had any relevance to current anthropology let alone the assignments we were supposed to do.Interesting class, but almost impossible.
Cute, fun, and good teacher

I have taken another anthropology class online, and I was hoping for another good experience with this one. However, the class is extremely disorganized. It may not be John's fault, but the TA never got back to students and it took days if she did decide to reply to an email. Assignments are unclear. Best to take in person if possible.

I took this course distance ed; HORRIBLY DESIGNED ONLINE, which really isn't John's fault. However, he is incredibly helpful but I do find it quite tedious to fit so much reading content into six pages, double spaced for the annotations. Take his courses in a classroom - he's so much better in person than on Webct! Otherwise, an interesting class!
Great class! lots of intresting movies, debate and lecture. Prof was amazing and very respectfull of everyone and thier stance on issues. Lots of reading, however its university so get use to it!

John is very approachable. LOTS of class discussion it can get very interesting. As mentioned, course is based HEAVILY on connections to course material. No test is sweet. Class attendance is MANDATORY if you miss a class you will be left behind (once a week four hour lectures). TONS of reading, make sure you have this in mind. TONS OF READING.

John is a rad guy! His class was interesting even though I dont care about SA at all. There are no tests so it's easy to do well if you're no an idiot. John is a Marxist, but generates good debates. He's open to your ideas, puts on loads of videos, and makes a 4 hr seminar do-able. I enjoyed the class, raised my GPA, and will miss him.
I guess he's a pretty intellectual guy who encourages a lot of class discussions. His class was pretty interesting full of videos to watch. Expects quite a lot from assignments, though.
easy to understand...not very challenging though.  very biased, but at least he admits it and so u know how to play along with him

Nothing great about Bogardus. Bias is his middle name. If you are socialist and an extremist you'll enjoy his class. If you have a scintilla of common sense you'll spend your money on different course.

It does not matter what sociology class he teaches;they are all Marxist classes. If you want to do well in them just dance to his tune and you'll get at least a B+. I made it through his socialist non-sense and I got a good grade but it was hell on Earth to listen to his Marxist babble.

Never met him cuz took online course.  Course was heavy with lots of readings but I did quite well and content was very interesting...probing u to think critically.  Next time I will try class setting as communicating online was confusing.  Essay structure works like other SA essay structures (unlike English courses...etc.)

NEVER take this class online!
 Very confusing and time consuming.
 I guess if you take the classroom format it would be ok.
 Enjoyed the material but did not enjoy the way the assignments are structured.
 If you want to do well, include as many quotes as possible in papers.
Best Prof ever! Take him...it will give you the opportunity to prepare and succeed in other courses. I still continue to use his methods as a grad student.

You either love or hate him.  I found this course frustrating.  While he preaches tolerance for all opinions, he really wants you to agree with his leftist/Marxist interpretation.  The only thing he looks for on written assignments are the NUMBER of quotations.  How is that real evaluation?
Interesting lectures, articulate, well-organized. Never a dull moment in class. Very personable and always willing to help out students.
Encourages independent thinking. The best thing about him is he is so approachable and respectful.

are we talking about the same guy? total time waster, 20 min. to vote about the seating arrangement for the day. WTF? i was dissapointed to see a prof of his feild demonstrate complete disregard for a students cultural tradition. practice what you
He's a really down to earth teacher.  I had lots of fun in his class, and it was eye opening.
No Comments
